Operation 'ap-approvals-readapprovalpolicy' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description lacks clarity on required inputs (policy ID) and what the response contains. The domain context is helpful but doesn't explain what this read operation returns. Enhanced description specifies the ID requirement and lists key response fields to help AI agents understand what data they'll receive.

data.current_description:
Read an approval policy object.

### What is an approval policy?
You can create approval policies at the organization-level for approving bills or vendor credits. 

When you create a policy, you can set rules, such as payment amount threshold, mandatory approvers, and minimum number of approvers. For example, you can set the payment amount threshold (`amountThreshold`) as `$1000` and minimum number of approvers (`minNumApprovers`) as `2`.

When you enter bills or vendor credits in the BILL web app, the Smart Data Entry (SDE) feature is used to auto-assign approvers that have been assigned to earlier bills or vendor credits. These approvers are also auto-assigned to new bills or vendor credits created with the API.

When you apply an approval policy to a bill or vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a specific approval policy object by ID. Returns the policy details including type, payment amount threshold (amountThreshold), minimum number of approvers (minNumApprovers), and associated mandatory approvers. Approval policies are organization-level configurations used to define approval rules for bills and vendor credits.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on required inputs (policy ID) and what the response contains. The domain context is helpful but doesn't explain what this read operation returns. Enhanced description specifies the ID requirement and lists key response fields to help AI agents understand what data they'll receive.

message: Operation 'ap-approvals-listapprovalpolicy' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Description references external documentation for pagination/filtering but doesn't explain what the response contains or what filtering options are available. Enhanced description specifies common filter criteria and clarifies the response structure to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description:
List approval policies.

See [Search operations with lists](doc:search-ops-with-lists) to learn about pagination, sorting, and filtering.

### What is an approval policy?
You can create approval policies at the organization-level for approving bills or vendor credits. 

When you create a policy, you can set rules, such as payment amount threshold, mandatory approvers, and minimum number of approvers. For example, you can set the payment amount threshold (`amountThreshold`) as `$1000` and minimum number of approvers (`minNumApprovers`) as `2`.

When you enter bills or vendor credits in the BILL web app, the Smart Data Entry (SDE) feature is used to auto-assign approvers that have been assigned to earlier bills or vendor credits. These approvers are also auto-assigned to new bills or vendor credits created with the API.

When you apply an approval policy to a bill or vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a paginated list of all organization-level approval policies. Supports filtering by policy type, vendor, and amount threshold. Returns approval policy objects with details including type, amountThreshold, minNumApprovers, and associated approvers. Supports pagination, sorting, and filtering as described in search operations documentation.
data.reasoning: Description references external documentation for pagination/filtering but doesn't explain what the response contains or what filtering options are available. Enhanced description specifies common filter criteria and clarifies the response structure to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'ap-approvals-getallbillapproverpolicy-v2i5o8' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, inconsistent_terminology. Summary contains '(UPDATED)' which is not appropriate for API documentation and is vague. Terminology inconsistency: uses 'Get a list' instead of consistent 'Retrieve' or 'List'. Enhanced summary is more descriptive and action-oriented. Enhanced description clarifies the scope (all policies), specifies response contents, and explains the use case for AI agents.

data.current_description:
Get a list of all approval policies.

In the response, you get details about whether each policy is for a specific vendor or for a bill amount threshold. In addition, mandatory approvers or approver groups are listed for each policy.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all approval policies with approver details
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a complete list of all organization-level approval policies including vendor-specific and amount-threshold-based policies. Returns policy details with associated mandatory approvers and approver groups for each policy. Use this operation to view all configured approval rules for bills and vendor credits.

data.current_description:
List approval policy approvers.

See [Search operations with lists](doc:search-ops-with-lists) to learn about pagination, sorting, and filtering.

### Who is an approval policy approver?
You can assign approvers to an approval policy that requires one or more mandatory approvers. In addition, you can set multiple approvers to a policy with `/v2/Bulk/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`. 

When you apply an approval policy to a bill or vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

**Note**: Replacing an approver in an approval policy is a two-step process.
1. Delete the existing approver from the policy with `/v2/Crud/Delete/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`.
2. Add the new approver(s) with `/v2/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`.

data.current_description:
Delete an approval policy.

In the response, `isActive` is set as `2` (Inactive).

### What is an approval policy?
You can create approval policies at the organization-level for approving bills or vendor credits. 

When you create a policy, you can set rules, such as payment amount threshold, mandatory approvers, and minimum number of approvers. For example, you can set the payment amount threshold (`amountThreshold`) as `$1000` and minimum number of approvers (`minNumApprovers`) as `2`.

When you enter bills or vendor credits in the BILL web app, the Smart Data Entry (SDE) feature is used to auto-assign approvers that have been assigned to earlier bills or vendor credits. These approvers are also auto-assigned to new bills or vendor credits created with the API.

When you apply an approval policy to a bill or vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.
data.description_suggestion: Soft-deletes an approval policy by marking it as inactive. The policy will no longer be available for new bill or vendor credit approvals, but existing approvals remain unaffected. Returns the deleted approval policy object with `isActive` set to `2` (Inactive). This is a soft delete operation; use the undelete endpoint to restore the policy.
data.reasoning: The current description is verbose and includes contextual information that should be in separate documentation. The key issue is that it doesn't clearly explain the soft-delete behavior, the impact on existing approvals, or what the operation returns. Added clarity about the soft-delete nature, side effects on new approvals, return value, and restoration capability for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Clear approvers for a bill or vendor credit
data.current_description:
Clear all approvers for your bill or vendor credit.

After the approvers are cleared, you can assign approvers with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

**Warning**: When you clear approvers with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json`, all approvers assigned to the bill or vendor credit are removed. This is regardless of whether the bill or vendor credit is approved, denied, or yet to be reviewed.
data.description_suggestion: Removes all approvers assigned to a bill or vendor credit, regardless of their approval status. Requires `objectId` parameter set to the bill or vendor credit ID. Returns a success response confirming all approvers have been cleared. After clearing, use `/v2/SetApprovers.json` to assign a new approval sequence. Warning: This operation removes approvers even if they have already approved or denied the document, allowing you to restart the approval process.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks clarity on required inputs, return values, and the specific use case (restarting approval process). Added input requirements, return structure, and clarified the warning about removing approvers regardless of status to help AI agents understand when and why to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Approve a bill or vendor credit
data.current_description:
Approve a bill or vendor credit.

Only users with approver permissions can be assigned to the bill or vendor credit for approval. If the bill or vendor credit requires multiple approvals, the next approver in the sequence receives an email for approval after a previous approval.

You can assign approvers for different use cases.
* Setting approvers for a bill or vendor credit with `/v2/SetApprovers.json` 
* Setting approvers for an approval policy with `/v2/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json` or `/v2/Crud/Update/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`
data.description_suggestion: Approves a bill or vendor credit by the authenticated user. Requires `objectId` parameter set to the bill or vendor credit ID. Only users with approver permissions can perform this action. Returns a success response confirming the approval. Side effects: If multiple approvals are required, the next approver in the sequence automatically receives an email notification. If this is the final approval, the document approval process completes. Use this when an authorized approver has reviewed and accepted a document.

data.current_summary: Deny the approval of a bill or vendor credit
data.current_description:
Deny the approval of a bill or vendor credit.

When the approval of the bill or vendor credit is denied, the approval process stops. After the reason for denying the approval is resolved in the bill or vendor credit, clear all approvers with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json` and then set approvers with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.
data.description_suggestion: Denies approval of a bill or vendor credit by the authenticated user. Requires `objectId` parameter set to the bill or vendor credit ID and optionally a `reason` for the denial. Only users with approver permissions can perform this action. Returns a success response confirming the denial. Side effects: The entire approval process immediately stops, preventing further approvals in the sequence. The document remains in a denied state until all approvers are cleared with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json` and a new approval sequence is established with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`. Use this when an authorized approver rejects a document.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks specificity on required inputs (objectId, possible reason parameter), return values, and critical side effects (approval process stops, document state changes). Added input requirements, return structure, and clarified the workflow for restarting approvals after denial to improve AI agent understanding.
